message:
chore: sync latest concurrent agent changes across runtime subsystem

Capture the latest batch of concurrent agent modifications including
runtime hooks, scheduler, lifecycle, conformance tests, and CI tools.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -107,7 +107,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-hwb.2","title":"Implement and verify outcome severity lattice plus budget algebra/exhaustion semantics","description":"Background:
Outcome joins and budget rules are central semantic contracts that must exactly match extracted behavior.

Subtasks:
- Implement outcome join and severity comparison operations.
- Implement budget composition, reduction, and exhaustion behavior.
- Add edge-case tests for empty/max/boundary and conflicting outcomes.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Unit tests demonstrate deterministic and associative behavior where required.
- Exhaustion semantics are explicit and failure-atomic.
- Matches canonical fixture expectations.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:09.895345522Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:11:34.192984085Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:11:34.192956734Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","invariants","math","phase-3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:09.895345522Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.560288881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb.3","title":"Implement transition authority tables for region/task/obligation/cancellation state machines","description":"Background:
State-machine authority tables are the primary substitute for compile-time typestate guarantees.

Subtasks:
- Encode legal transitions and explicit forbidden transitions.
- Enforce preconditions and postconditions on each transition API.
- Generate transition legality tests from invariant schema where possible.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Every lifecycle domain has explicit transition map.
- Illegal transitions return stable deterministic errors.
- Transition tests include positive and negative paths.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:09.976947939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:12:15.003704202Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:12:15.003678113Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","phase-3","phase-4","state-machine"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.3","depends_on_id":"bd-296.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.726175963Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.3","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:09.976947939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.3","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.643270002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.3","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:12:23.300454014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb.4","title":"Implement cleanup-stack and ownership/handle safety mechanisms","description":"Background:
C lacks RAII and borrow checking. We need explicit mechanisms to preserve lifecycle and aliasing discipline.

Subtasks:
- Implement cleanup-stack primitives for deterministic unwind and rollback.
- Implement generation-based handle validation and stale-handle detection.
- Implement optional debug quarantine mode for reuse hardening.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Cleanup behavior is deterministic and failure-atomic.
- Handle misuse paths are test-covered with explicit errors.
- Ownership assumptions are documented in API contract notes.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.058393232Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:25:55.844150859Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:25:55.844129940Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cleanup","core","ownership","phase-3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.4","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.058393232Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.4","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:30.148594467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.4","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:03:06.704593093Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.4","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.807706856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-hwb.5","title":"Implement ghost safety monitors for protocol and linearity invariants","description":"Background:
Ghost monitors provide lightweight runtime checks to recover part of Rust-level protocol confidence in debug/hardened modes.

Subtasks:
- Implement protocol monitor for lifecycle sequencing violations.
- Implement linearity monitor for reserve/commit/abort misuse.
- Integrate monitor outputs into test diagnostics and traces.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Monitor violations are deterministic and actionable.
- Overhead behavior is documented per safety profile.
- Tests demonstrate detection of known violation patterns.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.145870559Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:36:29.966237179Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","ghost","invariants","phase-3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.145870559Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:30.240868242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:03:06.611705988Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.888389910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:06:12.660062513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-hwb.5","title":"Implement ghost safety monitors for protocol and linearity invariants","description":"Background:
Ghost monitors provide lightweight runtime checks to recover part of Rust-level protocol confidence in debug/hardened modes.

Subtasks:
- Implement protocol monitor for lifecycle sequencing violations.
- Implement linearity monitor for reserve/commit/abort misuse.
- Integrate monitor outputs into test diagnostics and traces.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Monitor violations are deterministic and actionable.
- Overhead behavior is documented per safety profile.
- Tests demonstrate detection of known violation patterns.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.145870559Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:36:57.932299588Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","ghost","invariants","phase-3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.145870559Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:30.240868242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:03:06.611705988Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.888389910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:06:12.660062513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb.6","title":"Implement strict resource-contract engine and deterministic exhaustion behavior","description":"Background:
Resource exhaustion is a major C failure mode. The plan requires deterministic, non-corrupting behavior at all boundaries.

Subtasks:
- Implement configurable ceilings (memory, queue depth, timer nodes, trace events).
- Implement admission/backpressure/reject decision points with stable error codes.
- Add failure-atomic rollback semantics at boundary transitions.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No partial state corruption on exhaustion paths.
- Boundary tests cover arena alloc, queue growth, timer ops, and obligation ops.
- Behavior is consistent across profiles and reproducible in replay.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.229496469Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:35.789338346Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","oom","phase-3","resource"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-296.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:41.793538869Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-296.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:11.050816887Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.229496469Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.968580161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:06:12.747564647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:03:06.244601157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:03:06.336951536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb.7","title":"Finalize error stability policy, safety profiles, and deterministic time/entropy fault modes","description":"Background:
Error policy and deterministic boundary rules are core substitutes for Rust-level guarantees in C.

Subtasks:
- Define canonical error families and compatibility policy (stable meanings, additive growth rules).
- Implement compiler-enforced must-use behavior for transition/acquisition APIs.
- Implement zero-allocation contextual error ledger (`file/line/op`) for deterministic failure propagation breadcrumbs.
- Define safety profiles (debug-ghost/hardened/release-fast) with explicit invariants and overhead envelopes.
- Implement deterministic time/entropy/clock-anomaly fault injection hooks and fixtures.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Error policy is versioned and documented.
- Must-use and context-ledger behavior are test-covered and deterministic.
- Each safety profile has explicit invariants and intended use.
- Fault-injection paths are covered by automated tests linked to replay artifacts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.314994587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:57.824598446Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","errors","phase-3","profiles"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.7","depends_on_id":"bd-296.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:11.218064097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:10.314994587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:30.419783429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:57.824544074Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:06:12.838391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:03:06.427399380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:30.329649353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.7","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:11.135203071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb.8","title":"Implement adaptive-decision contract with expected-loss model, evidence ledger, and deterministic fallback","description":"Background:
Plan section 6.11 requires adaptive choices to remain explainable and replay-safe.

Subtasks:
- Define expected-loss decision framework and allowed decision surfaces.
- Emit evidence records for adaptive choices and confidence inputs.
- Implement deterministic fallback path when confidence is insufficient.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Adaptive behavior is auditable and replay-compatible.
- Fallback path is always available and test-covered.
- Decision outputs are stable enough for regression monitoring.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:53:10.016326127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:40.801676605Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adaptive","core","governance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.8","depends_on_id":"bd-66l.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:53:10.824987551Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.8","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:53:10.016326127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.8","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:53:10.724774356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
